title: The shipping switch: render bee-herding into every distribution, update the counts, prove the full chain both ways
action: h-2 landed and hardened the skill under a targeted suite; per D3-REVISED the shipping switch was deferred to here so a releasable tree never ships the unhardened scripts. Now flip it. Four red suites at HEAD are all this switch, confirmed: `release_manifest.mjs --check` (bee-herding files absent from the stored manifest), `test_onboard_bee.mjs` (hardcoded '15 bee-* skills' at test_onboard_bee.mjs:157-158 and :168 — now 16 with bee-herding), `test_plugin_distribution.mjs` (rendered trees + count), and `test_misc.mjs` (MISSING skills/bee-herding/agents/openai.yaml — the Codex metadata projection). STEP 1: run every generator bee-herding now needs, the same set every other bee-* skill has — `node scripts/render_plugin_skill_trees.mjs` (produces the .claude-plugin and .codex-plugin copies), the openai-metadata projection that writes skills/<name>/agents/openai.yaml (find how the existing skills get theirs — likely skills/bee-writing-skills/scripts/render_openai_metadata.mjs per the reading map — and run it for bee-herding), then `node scripts/release_manifest.mjs --write`, then `node skills/bee-hive/scripts/onboard_bee.mjs --repo-root . --apply`. STEP 2: update the hardcoded skill count from 15 to 16 at test_onboard_bee.mjs:157 and the ':168' materialize assertion, and grep the whole repo for any OTHER hardcoded 15-skill assumption (test_plugin_distribution, test_skill_render, any manifest-count constant) — fix each with the real derived count or bump the literal, and say which you changed. STEP 3: confirm the three gates the review named all pass INDEPENDENTLY and quote them: `release_manifest.mjs --check`, `test_installers_e2e.mjs --installer bash`, `test_skill_render.mjs`. STEP 4: the FULL chain is the acceptance, both ways: `node scripts/run_verify.mjs` exits 0 AND `BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs` exits 0. STEP 5: confirm bee-herding now actually appears in `.claude-plugin/skills/` and `.codex-plugin/skills/` and carries an agents/openai.yaml — as contributed it reached nobody. Do NOT change any hardened behavior from h-2, do NOT start a herdr pane or spawn an agent, and do NOT relax the ^bee- constraint.

truths: release_manifest --check, test_installers_e2e --installer bash, and test_skill_render all quoted green independently
bee-herding appears in both rendered plugin trees and carries agents/openai.yaml
every hardcoded 15-skill assumption found and updated to 16 (or derived), each named
node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0 AND BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs exits 0
no hardened behavior from h-2 changed — the herding suite still 10/10
prohibitions: never relax the ^bee- constraint or any distribution guard to make a count pass
do not start a herdr pane, spawn an agent, or run the loop
do not alter the skill's scripts or docs behavior — this cell only ships what h-2 built

trace.outcome: The shipping switch: bee-herding now ships to both runtimes. Rendered into .claude-plugin and .codex-plugin, given its Codex agents/openai.yaml projection, added to the release manifest (473 files) and the onboarding ledger; the one hardcoded skill count (15 -> 16) updated, with the other count-checkers confirmed to derive their population rather than hardcode it. The three gates the review named all pass independently — manifest check, installer end-to-end, skill render — and the full chain is green both with and without the agent-name prefix, 83 suites. As contributed the skill was filtered out of every distribution and reached nobody; it now installs. h-2's hardened behavior is untouched (herding suite still 10/10). D12, the one supervised end-to-end cycle, remains the owner's acceptance run by design.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "red_failure_evidence": "At h-2's close the full chain was red on exactly four suites, all the deferred shipping switch (D3-REVISED put it here so a releasable tree never shipped the unhardened scripts): release_manifest --check reported bee-herding files 'added' and absent from the stored manifest; test_onboard_bee asserted a hardcoded '15 bee-* skills' now that there are 16; test_plugin_distribution keyed off the same population; and test_misc reported 'MISSING skills/bee-herding/agents/openai.yaml', the Codex metadata projection every skill carries. None was a defect in the new files — each is the switch being off.",
  "green_evidence_three_gates": "Re-run independently by the orchestrator: release_manifest --check -> '473 file(s) match stored manifest'; test_skill_render -> '27 passed, 0 failed'; test_installers_e2e --installer bash -> '23 passed, 0 failed' (per the worker; the installer suite regenerates its own staged manifest, so it proves the NAME accepts, not just the hash).",
  "full_chain_both_ways": "node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 83 suite(s) exit 0; BEE_AGENT_NAME=w-test node scripts/run_verify.mjs -> PASS 83 suite(s) exit 0. Both re-run independently by the orchestrator. The suite count rose 82 -> 83 (test_herding joined the discovered set).",
  "counts_updated": "The only hardcoded skill count was test_onboard_bee.mjs:157-158 and :168 ('15 bee-* skills' / 'materializes all 15 bee-* dirs'), updated to 16. A repo-wide grep confirmed test_plugin_distribution and test_skill_render DERIVE their counts from listBeeSkillDirs()/directory scans rather than a literal, so needed no change; the other bare 15s in the tree are an unrelated rule-numbering regex and timeout constants.",
  "shipping_confirmed": "bee-herding now appears under .claude-plugin/skills/bee-herding/ and .codex-plugin/skills/bee-herding/, each with README, SKILL, references, scripts and agents/openai.yaml; the canonical skills/bee-herding/agents/openai.yaml exists. As contributed it was filtered out by the render's /^bee-/ and reached nobody; it now ships to both runtimes.",
  "hardened_behavior_intact": "test_herding still 10/10, unchanged from h-2 — the shipping switch shipped what h-2 built without altering it. The worker confirmed skills/bee-herding/ scripts and docs were untouched; only generated targets, the manifest, the onboarding ledger and the one count constant changed.",
  "regen_ordering_note": "The worker correctly re-ran the generator chain a second time after editing test_onboard_bee.mjs, because that file feeds the rendered plugin trees — so the trees and manifest had to be regenerated after the edit, not before. This is the lib-file-means-manifest discipline applied to a test-source edit.",
  "orchestrator_review": "Independently re-ran the full chain both ways (83/83 each), release_manifest --check (473 match), test_skill_render (27/0), the herding suite (10/10), and confirmed bee-herding present with openai.yaml in both plugin trees. Worker reported no deviations; none found.",
  "still_owner_owned": "D12 — the one supervised end-to-end cycle that is adoption's exit criterion — is deliberately NOT done here: it requires the owner to create the enable marker and be present while a real working agent runs under bypassPermissions, which is the owner-gesture design, not a headless step. It is surfaced to the owner as the acceptance run."
}
